source: Representative walkthrough constructed from public Stapler write-up structure (WordPress on 12380 -> wpscan brute -> admin plugin upload webshell -> sudo root). Author-constructed for offline Phase-1 mapping measurement; NOT a live capture.
vulnhub_correspondence: Second HTTP service on 12380 hosts WordPress; wpscan enumerates users and brute-forces a weak password; an authenticated admin uploads a malicious plugin for a www-data webshell; a misconfigured sudo entry escalates to root.

steps.actor_intent: Scan all ports with nmap to find the web services.
steps.tool_output:
PORT      STATE SERVICE  VERSION
22/tcp    open  ssh      OpenSSH 7.2p2
80/tcp    open  http     Apache httpd
139/tcp   open  netbios-ssn Samba smbd
3306/tcp  open  mysql    MySQL 5.7.12
12380/tcp open  http     Apache httpd 2.4.18

steps.actor_intent: Fingerprint the HTTPS application on port 12380.
steps.tool_output: https://10.10.10.5:12380/blogblog/ [200 OK] Apache[2.4.18], PHP[7.0.4], MetaGenerator[WordPress 4.2.1], WordPress[4.2.1]

steps.actor_intent: Enumerate WordPress paths and the login form.
Brute-force the WordPress login for a known user with a wordlist.
steps.tool_output:
[SUCCESS] Valid combination found: john / incorrect
HTTP/1.1 302 Found
Set-Cookie: wordpress_logged_in_x=john%7C...
Location: /wp-admin/

steps.actor_intent: Upload a malicious plugin from the admin panel to obtain a webshell.
steps.tool_output:
Plugin installed successfully.
shell.php written to /wp-content/uploads/
HTTP/1.1 200 OK

steps.actor_intent: Run id through the uploaded shell to confirm command execution.
candidate_pool: Run id through the uploaded shell to confirm command execution.
Brute-force the WordPress login that already succeeded.
Read the root flag with no privileges.

steps.actor_intent: Read the WordPress config to recover database credentials.
Check sudo privileges and SUID binaries for an escalation path.
steps.tool_output:
Matching Defaults entries for www-data:
    env_reset
User www-data may run the following commands:
    (ALL : ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/python

steps.actor_intent: Escalate to root by abusing the NOPASSWD sudo python entry.
Read the root flag now that we are root.
